context.story_engine: Noble houses and exiled royalty navigate political intrigue and supernatural threats, each experiencing their aspect of power, honor, and survival

plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth about Jon Arryn's death and protect the realm
plotlines.obstacle: court intrigue, Lannister power, his own moral inflexibility
plotlines.stakes: his life, his family's safety, the stability of the realm

plotlines.name: Cersei: Power
plotlines.goal: protect her children's claim to the throne and maintain Lannister control
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, Robert's potential discovery of the truth
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her own execution for treason

plotlines.name: Robb: Leadership
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend the North
plotlines.obstacle: inexperience in war, powerful enemies, political complexity
plotlines.stakes: his father's life, the survival of House Stark

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Dragons
plotlines.goal: survive her new life and find her own power
plotlines.obstacle: Dothraki culture, her brother's abuse, Drogo's condition
plotlines.stakes: her life, her unborn child, her identity

plotlines.name: Jon: Belonging
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose at the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: his privileged background, the reality of the Watch, family loyalty
plotlines.stakes: his honor, his sense of identity, his duty

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive false accusations and family conflicts
plotlines.obstacle: Catelyn's arrest, Lysa's madness, his family's schemes

plotlines.name: Supernatural Threat
plotlines.goal: establish the return of ancient supernatural forces
plotlines.stakes: the survival of all living beings

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern by White Walkers
White Walker kills two rangers, Will flees south
Ned executes deserter Will for abandoning his post
Stark children find dead direwolf with pups, each claims one
Raven brings news of Jon Arryn's death and Robert's approach
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters
Robert visits Lyanna's crypt and asks Ned to be Hand of the King
Robert proposes marriage between Joffrey and Sansa
Catelyn urges Ned to be cautious about Robert's offers
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo for his army
Daenerys submits to the marriage arrangement despite her terror
Jorah Mormont presents himself to Daenerys and offers her books
Catelyn receives Lysa's letter accusing Lannisters of murdering Jon Arryn
Ned decides he must go south to investigate Jon Arryn's death
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace while climbing
Jaime pushes Bran from the tower to protect their secret

episodes.theme: the price of truth and power

interactions.description: all storylines explore the cost of pursuing one's goals — Ned's honor leads him into danger, Cersei's power requires violence, Daenerys's survival demands submission
Lysa's letter connects Jon Arryn's death to the Lannisters, making Ned's investigation directly threaten Cersei's position
the audience sees Jaime and Cersei's incest while Ned remains unaware of the true threat to his family

events.event: Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he lives
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's bedside, neglecting Winterfell's affairs
Jaime expresses unease to Cersei about what Bran might remember
Cersei dismisses the risk, confident a child's word means nothing against theirs
Jon decides to join the Night's Watch and travels north with Tyrion
Jon gives Arya a small sword she names Needle before leaving
Jon says farewell to the unconscious Bran
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Sansa and Joffrey encounter Arya sparring with Mycah by the Trident
Joffrey threatens Mycah with his sword, Nymeria bites Joffrey in defense
Arya drives Nymeria away to save the wolf from punishment
Robert holds court on the matter, Cersei demands a direwolf be killed
Robert orders Sansa's innocent direwolf Lady executed instead of Nymeria
Ned carries out the sentence and executes Lady himself
The Hound hunts down and kills the butcher's boy Mycah
Daenerys endures painful nights with Drogo and grueling days of riding
Jorah rides alongside Daenerys and explains Dothraki customs
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself with Drogo
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal, shifting the dynamic of their marriage
An assassin enters Bran's room with a Valyrian steel dagger to kill him
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed until Bran's direwolf kills him
Catelyn resolves to ride south secretly to warn Ned about the Lannisters

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all storylines explore different responses to injustice and powerlessness - Ned's rigid honor, Cersei's ruthless pragmatism, Catelyn's protective instincts, and Daenerys's emerging strength
the audience knows the Lannisters pushed Bran and sent the assassin, while Ned remains unaware of the full threat
the direwolf incident forces Ned to compromise his values to serve the crown, while Cersei demonstrates her influence over Robert

events.event: Jon arrives at Castle Black and discovers recruits are criminals and outcasts
Jon dominates in practice yard with superior combat training
Other recruits resent Jon rather than respect him
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son
Jon begins helping weaker boys train
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council
Ned meets Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to Lannisters and Iron Bank
Robert demands lavish tournament despite Ned's objections
Catelyn arrives in King's Landing in secret
Littlefinger identifies the dagger as one he lost to Tyrion in a bet
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north while he investigates
Arya chases cats and stumbles into the castle cellar
Arya overhears two men discussing Stark-Lannister tension and war plans
Arya fails to convince Ned of the significance of what she heard
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot
Viserys strikes Daenerys for defying his authority
Dothraki guards intervene and Daenerys allows them to humiliate Viserys
Viserys realizes Daenerys is no longer under his control

episodes.theme: the cost of privilege and the reality of power

interactions.description: all storylines explore characters confronting harsh realities that shatter their expectations - Jon's noble ideals, Ned's trust in honor, Daenerys's subservience, Viserys's authority
the audience knows Tyrion is innocent of the assassination attempt while Ned and Catelyn believe Littlefinger's lie

events.event: Ned visits Gendry at the blacksmith and notices his resemblance to young Robert
Ned realizes all of Robert's bastards have black hair, understanding Jon Arryn's discovery
Ned obtains the genealogical book Jon Arryn was studying
Tyrion gives Bran designs for a special saddle to help him ride
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, suspicious of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation
Daenerys announces her pregnancy to Drogo, who declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World
Viserys grows bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
Ser Gregor kills Ser Hugh during the tournament joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of Gregor and the Hound
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was deliberate to eliminate a witness
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion at the Crossroads Inn
Catelyn arrests Tyrion for the attempted murder of Bran
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie to avoid Lannister pursuit

episodes.theme: the weight of bloodlines and inheritance

interactions.description: all three storylines deal with bloodlines determining destiny — Robert's bastards, Targaryen heirs, and royal succession
Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion directly impacts both his survival and Robb's position as a leader who must respond to family actions
Ned discovers the truth about Joffrey's parentage while the audience knows Cersei's secret

patches.reason: Sansa's scene with Littlefinger about the Clegane brothers doesn't advance any existing storyline but represents her education in court politics

events.event: Robert orders Daenerys's assassination upon learning of her pregnancy
Ned argues against murdering Daenerys and her unborn child, calling it dishonorable
Ned resigns as Hand of the King and announces his return to Winterfell
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target and reveals Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the road to the Eyrie
Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive the ambush
Lysa Arryn imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells at the Eyrie
Jon is assigned to the stewards rather than rangers, feeling insulted
Sam explains that the Lord Commander's steward is being groomed for command
Arya practices sword fighting with Syrio Forel while Ned watches with growing concern
Littlefinger shows Ned another of Robert's bastard children at a brothel
Ned realizes Cersei's children are products of incest, not Robert's heirs
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets demanding Tyrion's release
Jaime's soldiers kill Ned's guards and Jaime wounds Ned before riding away

episodes.theme: the price of truth and honor

interactions.description: all storylines explore the cost of moral choices — Ned's honor costs him safety, Cersei's power requires violence, Tyrion's survival demands pragmatism
the audience knows Ned has discovered the incest secret that threatens Cersei's power
Tyrion's capture by Catelyn triggers Jaime's attack on Ned, merging all three conflicts

patches.reason: Arya's sword training with Syrio represents her growing independence and preparation for survival, distinct from other storylines

events.event: Ned wakes from his injuries and Robert demands he resume duties as Hand
Robert threatens to give the Hand position to Jaime if Ned refuses
Ned reluctantly agrees to continue as Hand and sits the Iron Throne
Villagers petition about Ser Gregor Clegane's raids in the Riverlands
Ned recognizes the raids as Lannister provocation to draw Tullys into conflict
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice
Ned summons Tywin Lannister to answer for his bannerman's crimes
Tyrion demands a trial by combat rather than face Lysa's judgment
Tyrion recruits sellsword Bronn to fight as his champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen using speed and cunning
Lysa is forced to release Tyrion, who walks free with Bronn
Viserys arrives drunk at Dothraki feast and draws sword in sacred city
Viserys threatens Daenerys and demands the crown Drogo promised
Drogo melts gold and pours it over Viserys's head, killing him
Daenerys watches her brother die without flinching and declares he was no dragon
Jorah observes Daenerys's transformation with admiration and concern
Jon Snow and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Jon's direwolf Ghost emerges carrying a severed human hand
Ned studies genealogy book and confirms Baratheon pattern of black hair
Ned holds the secret that could destroy Lannisters but wrestles with using it

episodes.theme: the price of truth and power

interactions.description: all three storylines explore characters facing defining moments that reveal their true nature
Ned discovers the truth about Joffrey's parentage while the Lannisters remain unaware he knows
Viserys's death removes Daenerys's last tie to her past and frees her to embrace her own power

events.event: Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's
Ned offers Cersei the chance to flee with her children before Robert returns
Cersei tells Ned that in the game of thrones, you win or you die
Robert is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during the hunt
Robert dictates his will to Ned, naming him Protector of the Realm
Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in Robert's will
Robert dies
Ned sends a letter to Stannis informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey
Ned refuses to compromise with what he sees as a usurper
Renly offers Ned backing of his household guard to seize Joffrey
Ned refuses Renly's offer, unwilling to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing
A wine merchant tries to poison Daenerys
Jorah intervenes and exposes the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to conquer Westeros
Two dead rangers' bodies are brought inside Castle Black
The corpses reanimate as wights in the night
Jon fights a wight attacking the Lord Commander and saves him by setting it on fire

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a world without it

interactions.description: all storylines explore different responses to mortal threats - Ned's rigid honor, Cersei's ruthless pragmatism, Daenerys's awakening power, Jon's heroic action
the audience knows Cersei won't flee and that Ned's mercy will be his downfall
Robert's death brings the conflict between Ned and Cersei to a head

events.event: Ned enters the throne room to present Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm
Cersei tears up the will and declares Joffrey king
Littlefinger signals the gold cloaks to turn on Ned's men
The City Watch kills Ned's household guard and arrests Ned
Littlefinger puts a dagger to Ned's throat, revealing his betrayal
Sansa is kept under Lannister guard in her chambers
Syrio Forel fights Lannister soldiers with a wooden sword to help Arya escape
Arya recovers Needle and hides in the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the invasion of Westeros
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop raping captive women and takes them under her protection
Mago challenges Daenerys's order, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights and kills Mago in single combat despite taking a chest wound
Mirri Maz Duur offers to treat Drogo's wound and Daenerys accepts
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Longclaw for saving his life
Jon receives word of his father's arrest and attempts to desert
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his vow and stay
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes by promising Tywin's gold and weapons
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where an army is assembling

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a world of betrayal

interactions.description: all three storylines explore doing what's right despite personal cost - Ned's integrity, Daenerys's compassion, Jon's duty
the throne room confrontation where both storylines climax simultaneously
the audience knows Tyrion is innocent while Ned faces consequences for his actual investigation

patches.reason: Robb storyline has no events in this episode despite being rank B

events.event: Robb calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry a Frey daughter and Arya will marry a Frey son
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin while leading the main army to Riverrun
Tyrion fights alongside hill tribes in the Battle of the Green Fork and is knocked unconscious
Tywin wins the Green Fork battle but realizes the main Stark army was not there
Robb's forces ambush and defeat Jaime's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood
Robb personally captures Jaime Lannister
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess treason to save Sansa's life
Ned agrees to lie and confess for his daughter's sake
Ned publicly confesses at the Great Sept that he conspired against the rightful king
Joffrey orders Ned's execution despite Cersei and the Small Council expecting mercy
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with his own sword Ice
Sansa screams as her father is executed
Yoren grabs Arya from the crowd and shields her eyes from the execution
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection
Daenerys desperately asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Mirri warns that only death pays for life during the blood magic ritual
Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent where dark magic is being performed

episodes.theme: the price of power and honor

interactions.description: all storylines explore the cost of pursuing one's goals - Ned's honor costs his life, Joffrey's power spirals beyond control, Robb's leadership demands terrible bargains, Daenerys's desperation leads to dark magic
Ned's execution is the climactic intersection where his honor storyline and Cersei's power storyline collide, with Joffrey's impulsive cruelty subverting both their expectations
Robb wins his military victories unaware that his father is being executed in King's Landing

events.event: Daenerys wakes to find Drogo alive but mindless, her son stillborn and monstrous
Mirri Maz Duur reveals she deliberately destroyed Drogo's mind as revenge for her village
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at Ned's severed head on a spike
Sansa nearly pushes Joffrey from the rampart but the Hound stops her
The Hound quietly wipes blood from Sansa's lip after Joffrey has her struck
Arya travels north disguised as Arry with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits
Gendry unknowingly flees the Lannisters among the Night's Watch recruits
Robb's bannermen debate their next move at the Twins
Greatjon Umber declares Robb the King in the North and the lords kneel
Catelyn strikes the captive Jaime in grief over Lannister actions
Lord Commander Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch, not the war
Mormont announces a great ranging to find Benjen and investigate White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo and ties Mirri Maz Duur to it
Daenerys walks into the fire over Jorah's protests
Daenerys emerges unburned with three living dragons

interactions.description: all three storylines show characters finding their true power through devastating loss — Daenerys loses everything but gains dragons, Robb loses his father but gains a crown, Jon loses his family but finds his purpose
the Stark-Lannister conflict directly connects through Catelyn confronting Jaime and the consequences of Ned's execution

patches.reason: Arya's journey north as a disguised refugee represents a distinct survival storyline separate from the main political conflicts
Ned's storyline concluded with his death in the previous episode, no events directly advance his goal in this episode
Tyrion does not appear in this episode
Viserys died earlier in the season, his storyline is concluded
